items.title: Iran war casts Garuda Indonesia into even more turbulent skies
Tuareg rebels in control of Kidal
items.description: In today's edition, two days after a wave of coordinated attacks across the country, Mali remains on edge. Also, 42 people are killed in Chad after a clash between two families over a water point, escalated into a cycle of reprisals. And Kenya’s Sabastian Sawe smashes the two-hour barrier at the London marathon.

items.title: Fatal Hezbollah attack exposes gaps in IDF preparedness for first-person view drones
items.description: No proper defense exists yet for explosive drones guided by fiber optic cable, which can't be jammed electronically; Israel only began looking into solutions during current war The post Fatal Hezbollah attack exposes gaps in IDF preparedness for first-person view drones appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Thousands held by paramilitary RSF in Sudan’s el-Fasher: NGO
items.description: Hundreds of women and children among those detained by the rebel group, says Sudan Doctors Network.

items.title: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
items.description: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.

items.title: Starving Soldiers: Ukrainian Army's New Manpower Struggle
items.description: The photos of the emaciated soldiers circulated widely, along with complaints from angry relatives, prompting the removal of the unit’s commander. Four-plus years into war with Russia, it’s the latest controversy highlighting some of Ukraine’s continuing problems with logistics and manpower.

items.title: Mali’s militant attacks expose limits of Putin’s power in Africa
items.description: Russian backing for the ruling junta has not stopped rebel fighters striking significant blows in recent daysMali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence ministerWhen Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta, sat down with the Russian president, Vladimir Putin, in the Kremlin last summer, it s…

items.title: How the Iran war is bringing back 'citizenship as a weapon'
items.description: Some Gulf states, trapped between Iran, Israel and the US in the current war, have started revoking the citizenship of locals considered "traitors." But is it about security — or a way to suppress political dissent?

items.title: Libano, 'respingeremo qualsiasi accordo umiliante con Israele'
items.description: Presidente Aoun a Hezbollah, 'portare il Paese in guerra è tradimento'

items.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
items.description: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

items.title: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
items.description: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

items.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
items.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

items.title: Two Dead in Kramatorsk as Russian Shelling Hits Residential Area
items.description: A woman and a man were killed while standing on the street during a morning strike on the Donetsk region city, which also damaged eight homes and wounded a 50-year-old civilian.

items.title: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip, in blow to hopes for Iran war breakthrough
items.description: Peace talks between the U.S. and Iran were scheduled to continue Saturday in Pakistan, but they fell apart after President Donald Trump announced that he would not be sending U.S. envoys to take part in negotiations.

items.title: Russian Strikes Kill 6 Across Ukraine
items.description: Four people were killed and 27 wounded in a single strike on an apartment block in Dnipro. Another strike nearly killed a local official.
